title: Micromeles yunnanensis
taxonomy: Micromeles yunnanensis is a species of plant in the family Rhamnaceae. It belongs to the genus Micromeles and is a flowering plant. The Rhamnaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the buckthorn or dogwood family. Micromeles yunnanensis is a type of shrub native to China. This plant's classification is part of the larger taxonomy of flowering plants.

intro: Micromeles yunnanensis, also known as Yunnan hawthorn, is a type of plant in the Rosaceae family, characterized by its small white flowers and red berries. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its potential to lower cholesterol levels, regulate blood pressure, and act as an anti-inflammatory agent. The bioactive constituents of Micromeles yunnanensis include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often combined with other herbs to enhance their effects.
